claim: Vercel Eve is a filesystem-first TypeScript framework for building durable agents; Next.js for agents with folder-based structure
actionable: Use Eve for agent projects; structure agents as folders with tools, channels, and instructions for standardized workflows
source_title: You are going to want to build your Agents on this

claim: Model agnosticity critical; Fable's removal shows models can be taken away; use AI Gateway to route across OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini
actionable: Never lock agents to one model provider; implement model abstraction layer for switching without code changes
source_title: You are going to want to build your Agents on this

claim: Vercel Sandbox isolates agent code execution; sub-agents can run in parallel with different models for specialized tasks
actionable: Use Sandbox for safety; compose agents with parallel sub-agents for specialized workflows (research, generation, approval)
source_title: You are going to want to build your Agents on this

claim: Vercel Connect auto-discovers integrations for tools like Slack, Jira, Monday; can type tool name and find OAuth/API setup
actionable: Use Connect to add Slack, Gmail, Jira integrations; don't manually set up webhooks
source_title: You are going to want to build your Agents on this

claim: Discovery-plan-build cycle: research requirements, save to docs, create plan with Opus, implement with model of choice
actionable: Use structured discovery phase in agent development; reference project briefs in planning; iterate plan before building
source_title: You are going to want to build your Agents on this

claim: Agents can be deployed to Slack, web interface with Next.js, or scheduled via Vercel Workflows; all from same codebase
actionable: Build once, deploy to multiple channels; use Vercel infrastructure for scalable agent deployment
source_title: You are going to want to build your Agents on this
